cards.front: What is the rule for counting the number of ways to choose k items from a set of n items without repetition?
cards.back: The number of combinations is given by C(n, k) = n! / (k!(n-k)!)

cards.front: What is the rule for counting the number of ways to choose k items from a set of n items with repetition?
cards.back: The number of combinations with repetition is given by C(n+k-1, k)

cards.front: What is the binomial theorem used for?
cards.back: The binomial theorem is used to expand expressions of the form (a+b)^n

cards.front: What is the rule for counting the number of ways to arrange k items from a set of n items?
cards.back: The number of permutations is given by P(n, k) = n! / (n-k)!

cards.front: What is the rule for counting the number of ways to choose k items from a set of n items without regard to order?
cards.back: The number of combinations is given by C(n, k) = n! / (k!(n-k)!)
